title: Andryala rothia
intro: Andryala rothia, also known as Alpine Rock Rose, is a flowering plant species in the Asteraceae family, native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Andryala rothia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Andryala rothia is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to treat skin conditions and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Andryala rothia is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it among the flowering plants. More specifically, it falls under the genus Andryala, which includes several species of flowering plants. The genus Andryala is part of the tribe Anthemideae. Andryala rothia is native to the Mediterranean region, which is often associated with this family.
